svg.aria-label: The append-only event journal for run run-7f3a. A vertical sequence of events runs down the left column, each with a sequence number and a frozen timestamp: seq 0 run_started; seq 1 llm_decided for step 0 search_policy; seq 2 tool_result for step 0 with the policy text; seq 3 llm_decided for step 1 process_refund of 180 dollars to ORD-3300. After seq 3 a jagged break marks the crash: the process died after the refund posted but before its tool_result could be journaled, so there is no tool_result for step 1. On resume the tail re-runs and three more events are appended: seq 4 a second llm_decided for step 1 (the decision is re-made), seq 5 the tool_result for step 1, and seq 6 finished. A fold arrow on the right shows that state equals the fold over the log: replaying the events reconstructs which steps completed and their results, with step 0 memoized and step 1 re-attempted under the idempotency key so the refund is effectively once.
svg.title: The event journal: state is the fold over an append-only log, with a crash after seq 3 and a resumed tail

svg.text: One run, run-7f3a, with frozen timestamps. The log is the source of truth; to know what happened you replay the events, never trust in-memory state.
